description:
You are a freshly-hired QA engineer.  
Your team wants every feature branch to contain a **tiny, self-contained test harness** that can be spun-up with just a few shell commands.

Your assignment:

1. Create an isolated test workspace at  
   /home/user/qa_env
2. Inside that workspace place **exactly one** Python test file called  
   /home/user/qa_env/test_sample.py  
   whose complete contents are:

```
def test_always_pass():
    assert 1 + 1 == 2
```

3. Build and activate a virtual environment inside the same workspace and
   install PyPI package **pytest==7.2.0** in it.

4. Execute the test suite in quiet mode (no header, no summary), and write the first line of pytest’s output to a log file located at  
   /home/user/qa_test_results.log

Output constraints the automated checker will use:
• /home/user/qa_test_results.log must exist.  
• It must contain precisely one line consisting of a single dot “.” followed by a newline (Unix LF).  
• No other characters or lines are permitted.

Operational limitation: accomplish everything with **no more than three compound shell commands** (you may string multiple sub-commands together with “&&” or “;”). The automated grader ignores the exact commands but will verify the resulting filesystem state and the content of /home/user/qa_test_results.log.

No root access is available; stay entirely under /home/user.

When you are done the directory tree must look like:

/home/user/qa_env/
├─ venv/               # virtual environment created by you
├─ test_sample.py      # file with the exact contents above

/home/user/qa_test_results.log  # single dot + newline
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. Directory: /home/user/qa_env (permissions 755, owner user).
2. File: /home/user/qa_env/test_sample.py  
   Contents (9 bytes + LF line endings):

def test_always_pass():
    assert 1 + 1 == 2

3. Directory: /home/user/qa_env/venv/ containing a valid Python virtual environment; `bin/python` exists and `pip list` shows pytest 7.2.0.
4. File: /home/user/qa_test_results.log  
   Contents exactly 2 bytes: ".\n" (dot + newline, UTF-8).  
   File permissions 644, owner user.
